title: Elaphoglossum perelegans
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum perelegans is classified within the family Polypodiaceae. This family is commonly known as the polypody ferns. It belongs to the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that are characterized by their pinnately or bipinnately compound leaves. Elaphoglossum perelegans can be further classified within the genus Elaphoglossum, which comprises over 1,000 species of epiphytic or terrestrial ferns. The species is native to tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Elaphoglossum perelegans is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions, characterized by its delicate and slender fronds. Traditionally, Elaphoglossum perelegans has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Elaphoglossum perelegans is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create a cough syrup or expectorant cough drop.
